reply_text:
@ispadispad8529  I watched the game. 97% of the issue was on Team Serbia.  

In the first and second quarters, and even the third quarter, Serbia’s shooting was on point. Meanwhile, Team USA played terribly in those first, second, and third quarters. Honestly, in the third quarter, it really felt like Team Serbia was going to win, but then their shooting fell apart—completely pathetic in the fourth quarter.  

In the fourth quarter, Team USA switched tactics—LeBron locked down Jokic like a true defensive MVP, team USA defensive were on points, and their shooting percentage improved massively. On the other hand, Team Serbia went scoreless for the first 3 to 5 minutes, and the pressure completely got to them. They started making bad decisions and taking terrible shots.  

Yeah, there were a few missed calls, but let’s be real—the main issue was Serbia’s terrible shooting in the last quarter. Their shot selection and execution were awful, yet people are out here blaming the refs.
